budget_wines.name: Dr. Loosen Dr. L Riesling
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Widely distributed by Weingut Dr. Loosen, St. Johannishof, Bernkastel; sold across German supermarkets and international retail.
budget_wines.description: The benchmark entry Mosel Riesling: bright peach and citrus, low alcohol near 8.5 percent and a touch of sweetness balanced by slatey acidity.
budget_wines.tip: At under ten euros it is the textbook introduction to off-dry Mosel Riesling; serve cold as an aperitif or with spicy Asian food.

budget_wines.name: Selbach-Oster Zeltinger Riesling Kabinett trocken
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Direct from Weingut Selbach-Oster in Zeltingen and through specialist Mosel importers in Europe and the United States.
budget_wines.description: A dry village Kabinett from a 400-year Zeltingen family: peach and apple over slate minerality, dense juicy fruit and a long refreshing finish.
budget_wines.tip: The dry Zeltinger Kabinett is one of the Middle Mosel's great value benchmarks for fully dry Riesling at table-wine prices.

budget_wines.name: Markus Molitor Haus Klosterberg Riesling
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Direct from Weingut Markus Molitor, Haus Klosterberg, Bernkastel-Wehlen, and through international fine-wine retailers.
budget_wines.description: The estate-base bottling from a top Mosel name: clean slatey Riesling from the steep Klosterberg house vineyard, in dry and feinherb styles.
budget_wines.tip: Buying the entry Klosterberg is the cheapest way into a cult Mosel producer better known for its colour-capsule Auslese.

budget_wines.name: Moselland Bernkasteler Kurfuerstlay Riesling
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Moselland co-operative, Bernkastel-Kues; sold in supermarkets and through international retail including North America.
budget_wines.description: An honest, fruity, medium-sweet co-op Riesling from the Bernkastel collective site: lemon and apple, crisp and easy, the inexpensive everyday face of the Mosel.
budget_wines.tip: Co-op bottlings like this are the value floor of the Mosel; chill hard and pour as an undemanding party Riesling.

budget_wines.name: Moselland Riesling Hochgewaechs Steillage
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Moselland co-operative, Bernkastel-Kues; widely available through European retail and online.
budget_wines.description: A step up from basic QbA: a single-varietal steep-slope Hochgewaechs Riesling from the co-op, with riper fruit and more slate grip at everyday prices.
budget_wines.tip: Look for the Steillage and Hochgewaechs labels on co-op wine; they guarantee steep-slope fruit and higher must-weight at a friendly price.

budget_wines.name: Clemens Busch Riesling vom roten Schiefer
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Direct from Weingut Clemens Busch, Kirchstrasse 37, Puenderich, and through biodynamic and natural-wine importers worldwide.
budget_wines.description: The slate-coded estate Riesling from a biodynamic Marienburg grower: a dry, savoury wine capturing red-slate character at an organic estate's entry level.
budget_wines.tip: Busch's coloured-slate trocken wines are the affordable way into a serious biodynamic Mosel estate before its single-parcel GGs.

budget_wines.name: Immich-Batterieberg Detonation Riesling
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Direct from Weingut Immich-Batterieberg in Enkirch and through specialist Mosel importers in Europe and the United States.
budget_wines.description: The dry estate Riesling from a revived ancient Enkirch winery, named for the dynamite that built the terraces: fresh, dry and slatey from a serious address.
budget_wines.tip: Detonation is the gateway bottling at Immich-Batterieberg; a brilliant introduction to dry old-vine Enkirch slate Riesling.

budget_wines.name: Maximin Gruenhaus Monopol Riesling trocken
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Direct from Weingut Maximin Gruenhaus in Mertesdorf and through fine-wine retailers internationally.
budget_wines.description: The estate-level dry Riesling from the monopole Gruenhaus slope in the Ruwer: cool, precise, herb-and-citrus Riesling from a historic German vineyard.
budget_wines.tip: The Monopol trocken is the value entry to a centuries-old monopole estate; it shows the Ruwer's cool elegance without the GG price tag.

budget_wines.name: Melsheimer Riesling trocken
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Direct from Weingut Melsheimer, Dorfstrasse 21, Reil an der Mosel, and through organic and natural-wine importers.
budget_wines.description: A dry estate Riesling from a Demeter-certified Reil grower on steep Mullay-Hofberg slate: pure, low-intervention, savoury Riesling at an affordable price.
budget_wines.tip: Melsheimer's entry trocken is a great-value taste of certified biodynamic Mosel before stepping up to the single-site bottlings.

budget_wines.name: Heymann-Loewenstein Schieferterrassen Riesling
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Direct from Weingut Heymann-Loewenstein, Bahnhofstrasse 10, Winningen, and through fine-wine retailers in Germany and abroad.
budget_wines.description: The estate-blend dry Riesling of the terraced lower Mosel: powerful, mineral and fully dry from the Winningen slate terraces, an affordable Terrassenmosel face.
budget_wines.tip: Schieferterrassen is the gateway to Heymann-Loewenstein's dry style; at the top of the budget band but a genuine fine-wine bargain.
